description: Webcam stream displays in browser via getUserMedia
steps: Open http://localhost:3000
Allow camera permission when prompted
Verify live webcam video is visible on the page

description: Video element shows live feed without errors
steps: Open http://localhost:3000
Open DevTools console
Confirm no errors appear and video plays smoothly

description: Camera permission denial handled gracefully with error message
steps: Open http://localhost:3000
Deny camera permission when prompted
Verify a user-friendly error message appears instead of a crash

description: Start Session button activates coaching mode (starts both pipelines and recording)
steps: Open http://localhost:3000
Click 'Start Session'
Verify session timer starts and both video and audio pipelines begin

description: Stop Session button stops all pipelines and triggers upload completion
steps: Start a session
Click 'Stop Session'
Verify timer stops, pipelines stop, and Mux upload finalizes

description: Session timer counts up from 0:00 during active session
steps: Start a session
Observe the timer counting up from 0:00
Stop session and verify timer stops

description: After stop: app shows Processing... and polls /api/mux-asset until ready, then redirects
steps: Stop a session
Verify 'Processing...' message appears
Verify app polls /api/mux-asset and redirects to /playback?playbackId=xxx when ready

description: Canvas frame extracted from video element every 800ms during session
steps: Start a session
Open DevTools Network tab
Verify requests to Overshoot API happen approximately every 800ms

description: Frame sent to Overshoot as base64 JPEG with correct VLM prompt
steps: Start a session
Inspect a request to Overshoot
Verify request body contains base64 JPEG and the correct system/user prompt

description: Overshoot response parsed into VisualSignals type without crashing on malformed JSON
steps: Mock an Overshoot response with malformed JSON
Verify the app does not crash
Verify error is caught and logged gracefully

description: Signal overlay badges update with latest scores in real time
steps: Start a session
Observe the overlay badges (eye_contact, posture, expression, pacing)
Verify scores update approximately every 800ms

description: Overshoot fire:true signals route to debounce engine
steps: Start a session
Trigger a low score (e.g., look away from camera)
Verify the debounce engine receives the fire signal

description: Separate audio-only MediaRecorder captures mic in 5-second chunks
steps: Start a session
Open DevTools console
Verify audio chunks are produced every 5 seconds

description: Audio chunk uploaded to fal storage via /api/fal-proxy
steps: Start a session
Speak for 5 seconds
Verify /api/fal-proxy receives the audio chunk upload request

description: Wizper transcription returned with word-level chunks
steps: Start a session
Speak clearly for 5 seconds
Verify Wizper returns a transcript with word-level data

description: Transcript scanned for filler words correctly
steps: Start a session
Say 'um uh like you know' clearly
Verify filler words are detected in the transcript scan

description: Filler signal (>= 2 fillers) routes to debounce engine
steps: Start a session
Say at least 2 filler words in a 5-second chunk
Verify the debounce engine receives the filler_words signal

description: Live transcript strip shows last ~10 words, fillers highlighted in amber
steps: Start a session
Speak several sentences including filler words
Verify transcript strip updates and filler words appear in amber

description: /api/fal-proxy accepts POST with endpoint + input and returns fal result
steps: Send a POST to /api/fal-proxy with { endpoint, input }
Verify it calls fal.subscribe and returns result.data
Verify FAL_KEY is not exposed to the client

description: After session, Mux HLS URL sent to Gemini 2.5 Flash via fal-proxy
steps: Stop a session and wait for Mux playbackId
Verify /api/fal-proxy is called with openrouter/router/video endpoint
Verify the Mux HLS URL is included in the request

description: Report card JSON parsed and stored in Zustand
steps: Complete a session and wait for analysis
Open DevTools and inspect Zustand store
Verify reportCard is populated with overall_score, confidence_rating, top_strengths, top_improvements, summary

description: Report card rendered on /playback page with score, rating, strengths, improvements
steps: Complete a session
Navigate to /playback
Verify overall_score, confidence_rating, top_strengths, top_improvements, and summary are all visible

description: ElevenLabs WebSocket opens on session start
steps: Click 'Start Session'
Open DevTools Network > WS tab
Verify a WebSocket connection to ElevenLabs is established

description: Debounce engine triggers ElevenLabs speak() on threshold (3 fires in 5s)
steps: Start a session
Trigger the same signal 3 times within 5 seconds
Verify ElevenLabs speak() is called and audio is sent

description: Same signal suppressed for 15s after speaking
steps: Trigger ElevenLabs to speak for a signal
Trigger the same signal again immediately
Verify no second cue fires within the 15-second suppression window

description: Audio plays through speakers/earpiece correctly
steps: Start a session with earpiece/speakers connected
Trigger an ElevenLabs cue
Verify the voice coaching cue plays audibly

description: /api/mux-upload creates Direct Upload URL
steps: POST to /api/mux-upload
Verify response contains { uploadUrl, uploadId }
Verify MUX_TOKEN_ID and MUX_TOKEN_SECRET are used server-side only

description: Primary MediaRecorder (video+audio) streams to Mux upload URL
steps: Start a session
Open DevTools Network tab
Verify video+audio data is being sent to the Mux upload URL

description: Mux upload completes when session stops
steps: Stop a session
Verify the Mux upload request finishes successfully
Verify no data is lost on stop

description: /api/mux-asset polls and returns playbackId when asset is ready
steps: Stop a session
Verify GET /api/mux-asset?uploadId=xxx is called repeatedly
Verify it returns { assetId, playbackId, status } when ready

description: Each ElevenLabs trigger recorded as chapter cue with elapsed timestamp
steps: Trigger several ElevenLabs cues during a session
Stop the session and navigate to /playback
Verify chapter cues appear with correct elapsed timestamps

description: Mux player renders on /playback with correct playbackId
steps: Complete a session and navigate to /playback
Verify @mux/mux-player-react renders with the correct playbackId
Verify video loads and is playable

description: Chapter cue annotations shown as dots above the player timeline
steps: Complete a session with at least one ElevenLabs cue
Navigate to /playback
Verify colored dots appear above the timeline at chapter cue positions; hover to see cue text

description: Coaching report card (fal/Gemini) shown alongside the player
steps: Complete a session
Navigate to /playback
Verify the report card section shows overall score, confidence rating, strengths, improvements, and summary

description: Start New Session button navigates back to /
steps: Navigate to /playback
Click 'Start New Session'
Verify browser navigates back to /

description: Live signal overlay badges visible during session for all five signals
steps: Start a session
Verify badges for eye_contact, posture, expression, pacing, and filler_words are visible
Verify each badge shows a score

description: Overshoot malformed JSON does not crash the app
steps: Mock Overshoot to return malformed JSON
Verify the app catches the error and continues running
Verify no unhandled exception appears in the console

description: ElevenLabs WebSocket disconnect handled gracefully (reconnect or silence)
steps: Start a session
Simulate WebSocket disconnect
Verify app does not crash and either reconnects or silently skips TTS

description: fal-proxy errors returned as 500 with message, client shows error toast
steps: Trigger a fal-proxy error (e.g., invalid endpoint)
Verify /api/fal-proxy returns HTTP 500 with an error message
Verify the client displays an error toast
